title: ilex canariensis subsp. azevinho
intro: Ilex canariensis subsp. azevinho is a subspecies of the Ilex canariensis, commonly known as the Canarian holly or Azevinho, a flowering evergreen plant native to the Canary Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ilex canariensis subsp. azevinho are thought to include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
